query: My heading is rendering the literal text 'Conf\u00eda en nosotros' on the page instead of 'Confía en nosotros'. I wrote the accented letters as backslash-u escapes inside the JSX. Why is it printing the raw escape and how do I fix it?
expected_behavior: Explains that inside JSX text (between tags) a backslash-u unicode escape renders as its literal characters, not the accented letter
Fix: type the real accented character (á, é, í, ó, ú, ñ, ¿, ¡) directly in the JSX text
Notes that JS string literals — attribute values, JS expressions, .js non-JSX files — DO interpret the escapes, but recommends typing real characters everywhere for consistency
notes: Guards the JSX unicode-escape gotcha specific to Spanish UI text. A naive answer might blame encoding or the build; the skill knows JSX text nodes don't interpret backslash-u escapes and the fix is to type the real char.
